what_it_does: A command-line integration probe that spawns a supervised point-in-time clone of a multi-boundary source via runtime/cc_clone.py, sends the clone a direct message, checks that the reply reflects the past-point context and is not a model-resolution error, then tears the clone down and verifies the original source file remains untouched.
observations: Uses cc_clone.clone_at to materialize a clone at a specific compact boundary (default compact:1).
Accepts optional provider/model arguments or the environment variables COMPANY_PROBE_PROVIDER and COMPANY_PROBE_MODEL.
Sends a single DM asking what the most recent discussion topic was.
Explicitly rejects non-empty replies that are actually Claude Code model-resolution errors.
Verifies non-destructive teardown by checking the source file still exists.
Exits 0 on PASS and 1 on FAIL.
standouts: It is an end-to-end live probe, not a unit test: it spawns a real clone and talks to it.
The reply-validity check specifically guards against the false pass where a model error string looks like a reply.
The whole flow is framed around proving past-point context retrieval rather than the live tip.
conventions: Follows a sequential probe pattern: launch -> interact -> assert -> teardown -> exit code.
Uses sys.path.insert to import runtime.cc_clone from a sibling package.
Defaults are hard-coded to a specific local JSONL source path.
Breaks pure-assertion convention by printing narrative status lines and using the exit code for the test result.
concerns: Hard-coded default source path is environment-specific (/home/tim/...).
Model-error detection relies on substring matching of English error phrases, which is brittle.
No timeout or retry handling for clone launch or message round-trip.
ok_launch only checks presence of supervisor_session and source_untouched, not actual process health.
notes: This is an integration smoke test that proves a supervised past-point clone can be messaged and cleanly destroyed without harming its source.
questions: What does runtime/cc_clone.py expose beyond clone_at, msg_clone, and end_clone?
What is the exact format and schema of the source JSONL file?
How is supervisor_session validated, and what does a missing or false value indicate?
What does compact:1 mean in terms of boundaries or checkpoints?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to live-prove point-in-time clone extension with past-context answers and non-destructive teardown; the script does exactly that by orchestrating launch, DM, validation, and teardown.
intention_signals: It is trying to demonstrate that the fabric clone mechanism can resurrect a past conversational context, interact with it, and leave the original ledger intact.
novelty: The explicit anti-false-pass guard that treats a Claude Code model-resolution error string as a failure even though it is non-empty.
contribution: A runnable, self-checking integration probe that exercises the full clone lifecycle and encodes the specific invariant that replies must be real past-context answers, not model errors.
summary_for_embedding: Integration probe script that launches a supervised point-in-time clone from a JSONL source, messages it, validates the answer reflects the past context, and tears it down non-destructively.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a live end-to-end verification that fabric clones boot from a past point, respond from that context, and leave the source untouched.
